summary: engine/btree/persist.go adds a .root sidecar file (rootStateSuffix) mirroring NodeAllocator's .nodealloc idiom: SaveRoot does open/WriteAt(8-byte LE uint64)/Sync/close, LoadRoot does open/Stat(size-check)/ReadAt/close and returns reservedNodeID (0) with nil error when os.IsNotExist. Verified independently (not from implementer's plan/reasoning, per CDR isolation I4): fetched the literal issue #2 body via gh api and confirmed the 1.2.6 acceptance criterion and test spec text match the implementer's claim exactly. Read persist.go and insert.go's NodeAllocator side by side line-by-line -- SaveRoot/LoadRoot genuinely use WriteAt+Sync (not bare Write), 8-byte LE encoding matches NodeAllocator's own encoding exactly, and rootStatePath derives from store.f.Name() the same way NewNodeAllocator derives its sidecar path. Most importantly, read TestPersistReload line by line: it opens f via OpenIndexFile, builds a 400-key multi-leaf tree via real Insert calls (insertN, reused from 1.2.3's test helpers), calls SaveRoot, captures expected Lookup/PrefixScan results BEFORE closing anything, then explicitly calls alloc.Close() and f.Close(), and only then calls OpenIndexFile again on the identical on-disk path to produce a brand-new *os.File (f2) and a brand-new *os.File-backed NodeStore (store2) -- distinct Go objects, not a renamed alias of the original file handle or store. Confirmed via grep that engine/btree has zero package-level mutable state (no global maps/caches) that could let a stale in-memory structure leak across the close/reopen boundary and mask a persistence bug. LoadRoot(store2) recovers the root ID and the test then asserts full parity: every one of the 400 inserted keys resolves via Lookup(store2, ...) to its exact original fileID (not a subset), and PrefixScan(store2, ...) against 5 prefixes (including a 'nonexistent' negative case) is compared via reflect.DeepEqual against the pre-close scan results captured earlier, plus an explicit sort.SliceIsSorted check. TestLoadRootFreshIndexFile separately confirms a never-persisted index file's LoadRoot returns reservedNodeID (0) with no error -- the os.IsNotExist branch is real, not merely claimed. Rebuilt/vetted/ran the full engine/btree package myself (go build, go vet, go test -race -v -count=1): all tests across 1.2.1-1.2.6 pass together (0 failures), confirming no regression from prior subtasks. Confirmed the design choice to NOT call SaveRoot inside Insert/Delete is explicitly documented in both persist.go's doc comment (SaveRoot's 'Design note' paragraph, explaining the fsync-hot-path-cost rationale and that callers own committing a root) and in this run's architecture-discovery.md, so it is a stated caller-responsibility rather than a silently-assumed behavior. One documentation gap noted: docs/LLD/btree.md remains scaffold-level (still literally says 'Status: scaffold only') and does not mention persist.go, SaveRoot/LoadRoot, or the .root sidecar convention at all, despite persist.go's own doc comment pointing readers to it for 'full rationale' -- this is a pre-existing, already-flagged condition carried through every prior subtask's verification (1.2.4/1.2.5 runs note the same LLD staleness), not something newly introduced or worsened by this diff, so it is non-blocking here but should be addressed in a dedicated LLD-sync pass now that task 1.2 is fully complete.

requirements.evidence: Issue #2's checklist item 1.2.6 fetched directly via gh api and confirmed to read exactly: 'Acceptance criteria: A tree built, written to index/name.idx, closed, and reopened yields identical lookup/prefix-scan results as before closing. Test spec: go test ./engine/btree/... -run TestPersistReload: build tree, close, reopen file, assert lookup/prefix-scan parity. Impacted modules: engine/btree/btree_test.go' -- matches the implementer's claim verbatim. TestPersistReload implements exactly this: build (400-key multi-leaf tree via real Insert), write to index/name.idx-equivalent temp path, close (both the allocator's sidecar file handle and the main index *os.File), reopen (brand-new *os.File/NodeStore against the same path), and assert Lookup + PrefixScan parity against pre-close results. TestLoadRootFreshIndexFile additionally covers the never-persisted case, which is implied but not explicitly demanded by the issue text -- a reasonable, in-scope addition.

architecture_conformance.evidence: persist.go's .root sidecar mirrors NodeAllocator's .nodealloc idiom exactly: same directory as the main index file (derived from store.f.Name()), same fixed 8-byte little-endian uint64 encoding, same WriteAt+Sync durability idiom (confirmed via grep of insert.go's NewNodeAllocator/Next: nodeAllocSuffix, WriteAt, f.Sync() at the equivalent call sites). No changes to node.go's on-disk node format, and no changes to Insert/Delete/Lookup/PrefixScan signatures or internals -- purely additive, as claimed. SaveRoot/LoadRoot's signatures (store *NodeStore, ...) are consistent with the existing store-centric API surface established by Lookup/Insert/PrefixScan.

regression_risk.evidence: Diff touches only two new files (engine/btree/persist.go, engine/btree/btree_test.go) plus CDR bookkeeping; zero lines changed in node.go, lookup.go, insert.go, delete.go, or scan.go. Ran the full engine/btree package with go test -race -v -count=1: all tests from 1.2.1 (TestNodeSerialization), 1.2.2 (TestLookup), 1.2.3 (TestInsert*), 1.2.4 (TestDelete*), 1.2.5 (TestPrefixScan*), and 1.2.6 (TestPersistReload, TestLoadRootFreshIndexFile) pass together in one run, confirming no cross-subtask regression.

edge_cases.evidence: Fresh/never-persisted sidecar case (LoadRoot returns reservedNodeID, nil, not an error) is explicitly tested via TestLoadRootFreshIndexFile and independently confirmed by reading LoadRoot's os.IsNotExist(err) branch, which returns early before any size-check logic. A corrupted/wrong-size sidecar file (e.g. truncated by a crash mid-write) is handled defensively (info.Size() != rootStateSize returns an explicit error rather than silently misreading or panicking) but is NOT covered by any test in this diff -- a legitimate but narrow gap, since Sync() ordering makes a partial write of an 8-byte payload unlikely in practice but not impossible on an interrupted process. Recommend a follow-up test for this in a later hardening pass; not blocking for this subtask's literal acceptance criterion.

security.evidence: Sidecar file opened with 0o644 permissions matching NodeAllocator's existing convention; no user-controlled path components beyond the existing store.f.Name() (already trusted elsewhere in the package); no new external inputs or deserialization surface beyond the existing fixed 8-byte uint64 decode, which is bounds-checked via the explicit size check in LoadRoot before ReadAt.

performance.evidence: SaveRoot's open/write/sync/close-per-call design (rather than a long-lived handle like NodeAllocator) is explicitly justified in the doc comment: SaveRoot is expected to be called comparatively rarely (per-batch/per-checkpoint) relative to Insert/Delete, so a stateless cycle is an acceptable and reasonable tradeoff versus the added complexity of a long-lived handle. Deliberately not calling SaveRoot inside Insert/Delete avoids forcing an fsync on every hot-path operation, which is the correct performance-conscious choice given no acceptance criterion demands per-operation durability.

maintainability.evidence: persist.go's doc comments are clear, and the caller-responsibility design is documented both in-code and in architecture-discovery.md. However, persist.go's top comment points to docs/LLD/btree.md for 'full rationale' on the root-persistence design, but that LLD file was not updated as part of this diff and remains scaffold-level (still says 'Status: scaffold only', last_synced_commit predates 1.2.1) with zero mention of persist.go, SaveRoot/LoadRoot, or the .root sidecar. This is a pre-existing condition already flagged in prior 1.2.x verification runs (not newly introduced here), but since 1.2.6 is the last subtask of task 1.2, this is now the point at which the LLD-sync debt should actually be paid down rather than deferred again.

test_coverage.evidence: TestPersistReload asserts full parity across all 400 inserted keys' fileIDs (not a subset) plus PrefixScan parity across 5 prefixes including a negative/nonexistent case, with an explicit sortedness check -- matches the issue's 'identical lookup/prefix-scan results' requirement precisely. TestLoadRootFreshIndexFile covers the never-persisted-sidecar case. Not covered: a corrupted/wrong-size sidecar file, and a save-root-then-reload-with-a-second-write-before-save (i.e. verifying the reopened tree does NOT reflect writes made after the last SaveRoot) -- both are reasonable follow-up hardening tests but neither is demanded by the literal 1.2.6 acceptance criterion.

confidence.evidence: Directly verified the issue text via gh api (bypassing any risk of a paraphrase mismatch), read persist.go and btree_test.go in full line by line, cross-referenced NodeAllocator's idiom in insert.go to confirm the 'mirrors exactly' claim, confirmed no package-level shared state could mask the close/reopen test, and independently ran go build/go vet/go test -race -v -count=1 for the full engine/btree package myself with all tests passing. The task.jsonl entry for task-1.2.6 records commit 49f023c4c6217d1d17b45d896b11d207b2889939, which matches git rev-parse of the reviewed commit exactly -- no SHA correction needed this time (unlike the pattern seen in earlier subtasks).

explicit_determinations.sidecar_durability_writeat_sync: Confirmed: SaveRoot uses os.OpenFile + f.WriteAt(buf[:], 0) + f.Sync() + defer f.Close(), not a bare f.Write, genuinely mirroring NodeAllocator's Next() persistence idiom (also WriteAt + Sync) in insert.go. 8-byte LE uint64 encode/decode round-trips exactly (binary.LittleEndian.PutUint64 / .Uint64 are exact inverses).
explicit_determinations.true_restart_simulation: Confirmed as genuine, not a shortcut. TestPersistReload calls alloc.Close() and f.Close() on the original *os.File before calling OpenIndexFile(path) a second time to produce f2, a distinct *os.File, and NewNodeStore(f2) to produce store2, a distinct *NodeStore -- neither f2 nor store2 alias or wrap the original f/store. Confirmed engine/btree has no package-level mutable state (grepped for global vars/caches: none found) that could let stale in-memory tree structure leak across the close/reopen boundary and mask a real persistence bug. This is the single most important check in this review and it holds up.
explicit_determinations.fresh_never_persisted_case: Confirmed: LoadRoot's os.Open(path) failure is checked with os.IsNotExist(err) and returns (reservedNodeID, nil) -- not an error, not a crash. TestLoadRootFreshIndexFile exercises this directly against a real fresh index file (not a mocked path) and asserts rootID == reservedNodeID with err == nil.
explicit_determinations.scope_soundness_no_saveroot_in_insert_de: Confirmed intentional and documented, not an oversight: SaveRoot's doc comment explicitly states it is NOT called from Insert/Delete to avoid forcing an fsync on every hot-path operation, and explicitly assigns 'ownership of when to durably commit a root pointer' to callers as 'a policy decision, not something this storage-layer subtask should make on their behalf.' This tradeoff is inherent to the design and is the correct call for a storage primitives layer -- it is the caller's (a future write-path/WAL layer's) responsibility to call SaveRoot at appropriate checkpoints, and forgetting to do so causes stale-but-not-corrupt state (the last-saved root, not garbage), which is an acceptable and clearly-flagged risk rather than a silent bug.
explicit_determinations.lookup_prefixscan_parity_assertion_stren: Confirmed strong, not weak: Lookup parity checked against all 400 originally-inserted keys (full set, iterating the same map used to build the tree), each asserting found==true and fileID equality individually with the actual key/expected value in the failure message. PrefixScan parity checked via reflect.DeepEqual against the exact pre-close scan result (including a 'nonexistent' prefix expecting an empty/nil result) plus an explicit sort.SliceIsSorted check -- this is a full-fidelity comparison, not a count-only or first-N check.
explicit_determinations.issue_text_verification: Confirmed via `gh api repos/Aaryan123456679/HiveMind/issues/2 -q .body`: the 1.2.6 checklist item's acceptance criteria, test spec, and impacted modules text match the implementer's claim verbatim, character for character.
explicit_determinations.self_referential_sha_check: .cdr/index/task.jsonl's task-1.2.6 entry records commit 49f023c4c6217d1d17b45d896b11d207b2889939. `git rev-parse HEAD` on the commit under review (before the follow-up 9b819ad bookkeeping commit) matches this exactly -- no correction commit needed for this subtask, unlike a pattern seen in some earlier subtasks in this repo.

commands_run: cat engine/btree/persist.go (read in full, twice, once via Read tool once via sed to rule out a display artifact)
grep -n '^var ' engine/btree/*.go (confirm no package-level mutable/cache state)
cat docs/LLD/btree.md (confirm scaffold-level staleness, no mention of root persistence)
cat .cdr/index/task.jsonl | grep 1.2.6 (confirm recorded commit SHA matches reviewed commit)

non_blocking_findings.summary: docs/LLD/btree.md remains scaffold-level (still literally 'Status: scaffold only') and does not document the .root sidecar, SaveRoot/LoadRoot, or the caller-owns-commit-timing design, despite persist.go's own doc comment pointing to it for 'full rationale.' This staleness has been carried through every subtask of task 1.2 (flagged in the 1.2.4/1.2.5 verification runs too) and is not newly introduced or worsened here.
non_blocking_findings.recommendation: Now that task 1.2 (B+Tree) is fully complete across all 6 subtasks, do a dedicated LLD-sync pass on docs/LLD/btree.md covering the actual node layout, concurrency model as implemented (vs. as originally scaffolded), and the root-persistence sidecar convention, before starting task 1.3.

non_blocking_findings.summary: No test exercises a corrupted/wrong-size .root sidecar file (e.g. truncated by a process crash mid-write before Sync completed). LoadRoot does defensively return an explicit error for this case (info.Size() != rootStateSize check) rather than misreading or panicking, but this defensive path is unverified by any test.
non_blocking_findings.recommendation: Add a small follow-up test that writes a truncated (e.g. 3-byte) .root file and asserts LoadRoot returns a non-nil error rather than silently misreading a corrupted root ID.

non_blocking_findings.summary: No test verifies that writes made to the tree AFTER the last SaveRoot call are correctly absent/stale upon reload (i.e. that reload reflects the last-committed root exactly, not a newer in-memory-only root) -- the current test only checks that pre-SaveRoot writes ARE visible after reload, not the inverse.
non_blocking_findings.recommendation: Consider a follow-up test: SaveRoot, then Insert additional keys without a second SaveRoot, close/reopen, and assert the additional keys are absent (proving the sidecar genuinely pins the root at the time of the last SaveRoot call, not merely 'some root that happens to include everything').

recommendation: Safe to mark subtask 1.2.6 verified and commit/push as-is. The core adversarial concern -- whether TestPersistReload performs a genuine restart simulation rather than reusing the same in-memory file handle/NodeStore under a different name -- is confirmed to be real: the test explicitly closes the original *os.File and NodeAllocator sidecar handle, then opens a brand-new *os.File and NodeStore against the same on-disk path, with no package-level shared state to mask a bug. Durability idiom (WriteAt+Sync, 8-byte LE encoding) genuinely mirrors NodeAllocator as claimed. Fresh/never-persisted case is handled gracefully and tested. The no-SaveRoot-in-Insert/Delete design choice is a documented, deliberate tradeoff, not a silent gap. All tests across 1.2.1-1.2.6 pass together under -race with no regressions. Task 1.2 (B+Tree) is now fully complete across all 6 subtasks (1.2.1 node serialization, 1.2.2 lookup, 1.2.3 insert+split, 1.2.4 delete, 1.2.5 prefix scan, 1.2.6 persisted reload). The three non-blocking findings (stale LLD doc, no corrupted-sidecar test, no post-SaveRoot-write-staleness test) are appropriate follow-up hardening items for a subsequent pass, not reasons to withhold verification of this subtask.
